title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe | Easy Chinese Soup
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Hot and Sour Soup with our easy-to-follow Chinese recipe. Get the perfect balance of spicy, tangy, and savory flavors at home!

h2Headings: What is Hot and Sour Soup?
Ingredients for Hot and Sour Soup
Expert Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots, garnished with green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup – perfectly balanced with spicy, tangy, and savory notes.

description: This classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup is a symphony of flavors, perfectly balancing spicy chili, tangy vinegar, and savory umami notes. Loaded with tender pork, silken tofu, and earthy mushrooms, it is a comforting and invigorating dish perfect for any meal. Enjoy a taste of authentic Chinese cuisine right in your own kitchen with this easy-to-follow recipe.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 4 tbsp cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in hot water for at least 20 minutes, then drain, squeeze dry, and slice thinly. Dice tofu, shred pork loin, and prepare all other ingredients as specified. Mix cornstarch with cold water to create a smooth slurry.

instructions.title: Cook the Pork
instructions.text: In a large pot or wok, heat a small amount of oil over medium-high heat. Add the shredded pork and stir-fry until it is no longer pink, about 2-3 minutes. Remove the cooked pork and set aside.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Vegetables
instructions.text: Pour the chicken broth into the pot. Bring it to a boil, then add the rehydrated shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Reduce heat to medium-low and let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Season and Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and white pepper. Return the cooked pork to the pot, then add the diced firm tofu. Bring the soup back to a gentle simmer. Slowly stir in the cornstarch slurry, mixing constantly until the soup begins to thicken to your desired consistency. Add more slurry if a thicker soup is preferred.

instructions.title: Add Egg and Finish
instructions.text: Once the soup is thickened, slowly pour the beaten egg into the simmering soup in a thin stream while continuously stirring clockwise with a fork or chopsticks. This creates fine egg ribbons. Turn off the heat immediately. Stir in the sesame oil.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into individual bowls. Garnish generously with chopped green onions. For an extra kick, drizzle a little chili oil over each serving. Serve immediately and enjoy this authentic Chinese delicacy.

tips: Achieve the perfect balance of hot and sour by adjusting the white pepper and rice vinegar to your personal preference at the end of cooking. Always taste and adjust.
For a silky smooth egg flower, ensure the soup is simmering gently, pour the beaten egg in a very thin stream, and stir slowly in one direction to create delicate strands.
Always rehydrate dried mushrooms properly. The soaking liquid can be strained and added to the broth for an extra layer of umami flavor.
article: Hot and Sour Soup is a beloved Chinese Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
